aboutBlock.desc:
My name is Sergey, and I have over 3 years of experience in Frontend development. My main stack includes React, TypeScript, Redux (including Redux Toolkit and Thunk), as well as Next.js and React Native for mobile development.

I pay special attention to website performance optimization (Lighthouse, SEO), writing tests, and developing complex user components. In my work, I actively use UI libraries (Ant Design, MUI, Mantine UI, PrimeReact) and have experience working with various APIs (REST, GraphQL, third-party services).

I created this portfolio website to showcase my skills and completed projects :) It's built using my main work stack and serves as an example of my capabilities.

I'm confident that my experience and skills will be valuable to your team. I'd be happy to discuss my candidacy and answer any questions during an interview.

block1.desc: SPA and SSR applications on React/Next.js. I create adaptive layouts (CSS Modules, SCSS, Tailwind), optimize loading (Lighthouse ≥90). Experience with REST/GraphQL APIs.

block2.desc: React + TypeScript, state management (Redux/Zustand/Effector). Worked with Material UI, Ant Design, Prime React. Build projects with Vite/Webpack. Testing: Jest + React Testing Library.

block3.desc: Prototypes in Figma, adaptive grids, micro-interactions (Framer Motion). Knowledge of WCAG principles. Can explain why 'beautiful' ≠ 'convenient'.

block4.desc: Git (branches, rebase, conflicts), Code Review, ESLint/Prettier. Task decomposition in Jira/YouTrack. Docker for local dev environment.

block1.name: Grigory Ilkevich
block1.review: Sergey made a website and app for us - and for six months now I haven't heard a single 'where's the button?' from our investors. The design is convenient, the code is fire, and most importantly: he suggested many features we hadn't even thought about. Works like Swiss watches, only cheaper 😄

block2.name: Dmitry Rabkin
block2.review: Sergey was hired to the team with doubts - 'Another frontender...'. But within a month everyone was asking: 'Where's that Sergey? Let him fix our module too.' Made the interface so logical that even our accountant figured it out without instructions. A rare case when a developer thinks about the user BEFORE being reminded.

block3.name: Lev Podelnikov
block3.review: Ordered a business card site... got a 'wow' site. Sergey managed to include animations that don't lag, adaptivity for all phones (tested on grandpa's Nokia!), and even a dark theme - though we didn't ask for it. Now all clients ask: 'Who's your designer?'. Too bad he can't be cloned 🤷‍♂️

block2.h: Developed SPA on React from scratch

block4.h: Pump not just code but also muscles

block1.desc:
Developed SPA on Next.js from scratch (50+ pages): SSR/SSG for SEO, dynamic routes, multilingualism (i18n).

Key implementations: role system with JWT authorization, video platform with custom player, interactive dashboards with charts (Chart.js).

Performance optimization: reduced TTI by 35% via code splitting + lazy loading, decreased bundle size by 30% (analysis via Webpack Bundle Analyzer), configured API request caching (SWR).

Processes and collaboration: set up CI/CD (GitHub Actions), close work with CEO: proposed business logic solutions (increased conversion by 20%), received offer to become product co-owner for significant contribution.

Stack: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, Redux Thunk, MUI, CSS Modules

block2.desc:
Developed interfaces for dormitory management system.

Key tasks: teamwork with 3 frontenders (code review, git flow), legacy code refactoring, TypeScript implementation, dev/prod environment separation setup.

Stack: React, JavaScript/TypeScript, Prime React, SCSS, GitLab CI

block3.desc:
Taught beginner courses: JavaScript basics and algorithms, DOM and browser APIs, practical task development on real cases.

Achievements: improved skills in explaining complex concepts simply, developed interactive examples

block4.desc:
Commercial projects: landing pages and SPAs on Vue/React, experiments with Three.js for 3D graphics.

Acquired skills: working with client requirements and deadlines, quick learning of new technologies.

Stack: Vue 2/3, React, JavaScript, Tailwind ...

block1.name: Bauman Moscow State Technical University
block1.desc:
In-depth computer science: OOP and SOLID principles, algorithms and data structures (hash tables, graphs, trees, O-notation complexity).
Applied mathematics: working with matrices, linear regression, data interpolation/extrapolation (implementation in JavaScript/TypeScript).
Low-level knowledge: computer architecture, memory work, performance optimization.
Network technologies: HTTP/HTTPS, WebSockets, REST API principles.
Parallel programming: multithreading, asynchrony (Event Loop).

block2.name: Gymnasium №7
block2.desc:
Basic programming skills in C++ and Python.
Robotics: microcontroller programming (Arduino, LEGO Mindstorms).
Introduction to Linux systems and PC hardware.
Participation in math and computer science olympiads.

categories.title: Styling & UI
items: Libraries: Material-UI (MUI), Ant Design, Mantine UI, PrimeReact

knowledgesArr: Modern CSS methodologies (CSS Modules, SCSS, Tailwind)
CI/CD and automation (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
Working with design systems (Material UI, Ant Design)

projects.text1:
Large-scale Next.js application with 50+ pages and the following key technologies:

- Full-fledged JWT authorization with token storage in localStorage
- Role-based access system (RBAC) with different permission levels
- Multilingualism with i18n (Russian/English/Estonian)
- Component architecture: custom components + adapted Material UI
- Comprehensive data visualization with Chart.js (line charts, doughnut charts)
- Interactive tables with dataset filtering
- State management: Redux + Redux Thunk for async operations
- Optimizations: SWR for caching, lazy loading, SSR
- Testing: Jest unit tests
- SEO: Lighthouse optimization
- Custom build setup via Webpack
- CI/CD: Docker + Nginx setup, automatic deployment via GitHub Actions
projects.text2:
Key achievements:

1. Implemented high-load authorization system with JWT and role model
2. Optimized performance via SSR and code-splitting (Lazy Loading)
3. Created comprehensive dashboard analytics with interactive Chart.js visualizations
4. Set up full CI/CD pipeline with Docker containerization
5. Achieved 90+ Lighthouse scores on all key metrics
6. Implemented multilingualism with dynamic translation loading

Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, RTK Query, Material UI v5, Chart.js 4, i18next, Jest, Docker, Nginx

projects.text1:
Cross-platform React Native app (Expo) with full functional identity to web version. Key development aspects:

- Full functionality synchronization with Next.js web version
- JWT authorization adaptation for mobile platform
- Same role system implementation (RBAC) considering mobile UX
- Multilingual support (i18n) with shared translation config
- Custom solutions to replace unsupported libraries:
  - Deep native module modifications
  - Polyfills for web-specific APIs
  - Rewriting critical library parts (Chart.js → Victory Native)
- Performance optimization for low-end devices
projects.text2:
Technical challenges and solutions:

1. Migration from web-specific libraries to mobile alternatives:
   - Material UI → React Native Paper + custom components
   - Chart.js → Victory Native with business logic adaptations
   - Redux Persist → AsyncStorage with custom serialization

2. Native improvements:
   - Gesture implementation for interactive charts
   - Large dataset handling optimization
   - Custom navigation stack (React Navigation 6)

3. Implementation features:
   - Single codebase for iOS/Android
   - Offline mode with critical data caching
   - Push notifications via Expo Notifications
   - Biometric authorization

Stack: React Native 0.72, Expo SDK 48, TypeScript, Victory Native, React Navigation 6, Redux Toolkit, i18next, Jest

projects.text1:
Personal SPA resume with full custom stack and the following key features:

- Architecture: Vite + React (no UI libraries)
- 100% custom components (Zero Dependency UI)
- Performance:
  - Vite build (5x faster than Webpack)
  - Optimization for low-end servers (1GB RAM)
  - Code splitting + tree shaking
- Internationalization:
  - i18n with dynamic language loading (RU/EN)
  - SEO-optimized meta tags for each language
- Interactivity:
  - Custom SwiperJS implementation
  - Complex animations via Animate.css
  - Parallax effects (pure CSS)
projects.text2:
Technical achievements and features:

1. Performance:
   - Build time <15s (vs 2.5min in Webpack)
   - Lighthouse Score: 98/100 (PWA-ready)
   - Lazy-loading of all heavy resources

2. Unique implementations:
   - Adaptive grid system without third-party libraries

3. Animations and UX:
   - Micro-animations for all interactive elements
   - Smooth page transitions

Stack: Vite 5, React 18, TypeScript, i18next, Animate.css, SwiperJS 11, CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

projects.text1:
Next.js application for house rentals with full SSR rendering and the following key technologies:

- Architecture: 100% server-side data (except PATCH/POST requests)
- UI Kit: Mantine UI with deep component customization
- Image optimization:
  - Custom carousel on SwiperJS (v11)
  - Next.js Image with lazy loading
  - Content loading prioritization (Critical Images)
- Security: Google Authenticator for admin panel (6-digit TOTP)
- Validation: complex schemas for all forms (Zod + React Hook Form)
- SEO: 95+ Lighthouse scores (SSR + dynamic meta tags)
projects.text2:
Admin system and technical features:

1. Protected admin panel:
   - TOTP authentication (google-authenticator)
   - JWT tokens with short TTL
   - Custom data validation (including files)

2. Optimizations:
   - Dynamic SwiperJS import
   - Prefetch for critical routes
   - API response caching (stale-while-revalidate)

3. Integrations:
   - Tabler Icons (custom build with only used icons)
   - Server Actions for data mutations
   - Streaming SSR for slow connections

4. Implementation features:
   - Zero-client state management (using server cache)
   - Progressive enhancement for old browsers
   - Full type-safety (TypeScript strict mode)

Stack: Next.js 14 (App Router), Mantine UI 7, SwiperJS 11, Zod, React Hook Form, Google Authenticator, Tabler Icons, Turbopack

projects.header: Landing Page for Heat Power Company
projects.text1:
High-performance Next.js landing page with full deployment infrastructure:

- Next.js 14 with SSR for instant content loading
- Automatic meta tag generation for SEO
- Optimized media handling:
  - Compressed WebP images
  - Lazy loading of below-the-fold content
- Full infrastructure:
  - Docker containerization
  - Nginx as reverse proxy with HTTP/2
  - Let's Encrypt certificates via Certbot
- CI/CD pipeline:
  - Automatic deployments via GitHub Actions
  - Build verification before deployment
  - Dependency caching
projects.text2:
Technical implementation features:

1. Performance:
   - 100/100 Google PageSpeed scores
   - TTFB < 200ms thanks to SSR
   - Optimized web fonts

2. Security:
   - Automatic SSL updates
   - CSP security headers
   - DDoS protection via Nginx rate-limiting

3. DevOps solutions:
   - Multi-stage Docker build
   - Container health checks
   - Service auto-recovery

Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Docker 24, Nginx 1.25, Certbot 2.8, CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

projects.text1:
An innovative React+Vite app where I sell myself at a premium price (but with a discount):

- Architecture: 'A4-first design' (no extra pixels allowed!)
- UI Kit: CSS Modules + 3 hours wrestling with `margin: 0 auto`
- Optimization: PDF generation without Word (because 'I'm a frontend dev, not office plankton')
- Security: i18n protects against HRs who don't know English
- Validation: The 'Download' button checks if you have a PDF reader
- SEO: 100 Lighthouse score (because there's nothing there except the resume)
projects.text2:
Technical features:

1. Foolproofing:
   - The 'Change Language' button requires confirmation ('Are you sure? You only studied English in school!')
   - The PDF generator ignores typos in the resume (thanks to the 'AutoBeauty' magic algorithm)

2. Optimizations:
   - Lazy loading of Word jokes (to avoid immediate load)
   - Prefetching all my future projects (while they don't exist yet)
   - Caching self-confidence in my skills (TTL = ∞)

3. Integrations:
   - GitHub API to count stars (0 → but I'm an optimist!)
   - LinkedIn API for automatic 'Open to Work' (self-initiated)

4. Implementation quirks:
   - 90% of time spent on PDF rendering setup (but at least it's not Word!)
   - 10% convincing myself 'this isn't overengineering'
   - 0% on documentation (but there's a ReadME!)

Tech stack: React 18, Vite 5, i18n (for the 'Me/Not Me' toggle button), PDF-lib (revenge against Word), 3 cups of coffee.
